# Snapie Auth — LLM Integration Reference
# Version: 1.6 | Updated: 2026-06-13
# Support: https://discord.gg/CgJP7t7nWy
# Base URL: https://auth.snapie.io/api

Snapie Auth is a Hive blockchain account gateway and signing proxy.
It authenticates users via Google OAuth, Sign in with Apple, or email/password,
creates custodial Hive accounts, and signs Hive operations on behalf of users.

## Authentication Model

All user-facing endpoints require a session cookie (`snapieauth_session`)
set by the server after login. State-mutating endpoints also require the
`x-csrf-token` header, read from the JS-accessible `snapieauth_csrf` cookie.

There is NO Bearer token for user APIs. The Internal API (service-to-service)
uses `Authorization: Bearer <INTERNAL_API_KEY>`.

Always include `credentials: 'include'` in fetch calls.

## Key Concepts

CUSTODIAL: Snapie generates all 4 Hive keypairs server-side (owner, active,
posting, memo), derived from a single master password via Hive's fromLogin.
The master password and keys are AES-256-GCM encrypted with a server-derived
key. The user never sees or holds them until voluntary emancipation, at which
point both the master password and all 4 keys are returned.

EMANCIPATED: User holds their own keys. Snapie only has posting authority
(granted by the user). Active-level ops return:
  { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp: [...], account: "alice", keyType: "active" }
Sign-message returns:
  { needsClientSigning: true, message: "...", account: "alice", keyType: "posting" }
The consuming app should hand these fields directly to Aioha or Keychain.

SPONSOR TOKEN: A one-time token tied to an email hash. When redeemed, it
bypasses the daily free-account quota (global + per-IP) and the
"previously had account" restriction. The account still consumes one ACT.
Issued by admins via /admin.html or the Admin API, or by external apps
via the Internal API.

EMANCIPATION THRESHOLD: Configurable USD value. When a custodial account
exceeds this value, the user must export their keys before continuing.
Set EMANCIPATION_THRESHOLD_USD=0 to disable.

FREE ACCOUNT QUOTA: By default 10 accounts/day globally and 2/IP/day.
Check remaining slots with GET /quota (public, no auth). Sponsor tokens
bypass this limit entirely.

ACCOUNT CREATION FLOW:
1. Check GET /quota — if remaining > 0, user can proceed (or if sponsored/paid)
2. POST /account/create → returns jobId
3. Poll GET /account/job/:jobId every 3-5 seconds
4. Status: pending → broadcasting → acked → confirmed (or failed/expired)
5. On confirmed: user.hiveUsername is set

PAID ACCOUNT CREATION FLOW (no KYC):
Account creation fee is always the live witness median — never hardcoded.
Two payment rails, both deposit HIVE to the operator's account:

HIVE payment:
1. GET /payment/fee → { amountHive, amountUsd }
2. POST /payment/hive-intent → { memo, receivingAccount, amount, expiresAt }
3. User sends exactly `amount` HIVE to `receivingAccount` with `memo` as transfer memo
4. Poll GET /payment/intent/:memo every 3-5 seconds
5. On confirmed: POST /account/create proceeds (paid flag bypasses daily quota)

Lightning payment (via v4v.app — converts sats → HIVE, sends to same account):
1. GET /payment/fee → { amountHive, amountUsd }
2. POST /payment/lightning-intent → { memo, invoice, amountUsd, expiresAt }
3. Display `invoice` as QR code for user's Lightning wallet
4. Poll GET /payment/intent/:memo every 3-5 seconds
5. On confirmed: POST /account/create proceeds

Intent statuses: pending | confirmed | expired (TTL 30 min)

## Quick Start Integration

```javascript
// 1. Check quota before sending users to register
const quota = await fetch('/api/quota').then(r => r.json())
// { total: 10, used: 3, remaining: 7, resetsAt: "2026-06-09T00:00:00.000Z" }
if (quota.remaining === 0) {
  // show "no slots today — request a sponsor invite or try tomorrow"
}

// 2a. Google login — returns { user: SnapieUser } directly, no extra /auth/me needed
async function loginWithGoogle(idToken) {
  const res = await fetch('/api/auth/google', {
    method: 'POST',
    credentials: 'include',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ credential: idToken })
  })
  const { user } = await res.json()
  // user.hiveUsername is null until account is created
  return user
}

// 2b. Apple Sign-In — identityToken from ASAuthorizationAppleIDCredential.
// IMPORTANT: iOS only provides name on the very first sign-in. Pass it then; omit on subsequent logins.
// The server preserves the stored name if name is absent.
async function loginWithApple(identityToken, name = null) {
  const res = await fetch('/api/auth/apple', {
    method: 'POST',
    credentials: 'include',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ identityToken, name })
    // name: { firstName: "John", lastName: "Doe" } | null
  })
  const { user } = await res.json()
  return user
}

// Email login works the same way
async function loginWithEmail(email, password) {
  const res = await fetch('/api/auth/email/login', {
    method: 'POST',
    credentials: 'include',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({ email, password })
  })
  if (res.status === 403) throw new Error('email_not_verified')
  if (!res.ok) throw new Error((await res.json()).error)
  const { user } = await res.json()
  return user
}

// Call GET /auth/me only when you need email or account value info,
// or to re-validate an existing session on page load.
async function getMe() {
  const res = await fetch('/api/auth/me', { credentials: 'include' })
  if (!res.ok) return null
  return (await res.json()).user
}

// 3. Read CSRF token
function getCsrf() {
  const m = document.cookie.match(/snapieauth_csrf=([^;]+)/)
  return m ? decodeURIComponent(m[1]) : null
}

// 4. Authenticated API call
async function api(method, path, body) {
  const res = await fetch(`/api${path}`, {
    method,
    credentials: 'include',
    headers: {
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      'x-csrf-token': getCsrf()
    },
    body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined
  })
  return res.json()
}

// 5. Create a custodial Hive account
const { jobId } = await api('POST', '/account/create', {
  username: 'alice',
  custodyMode: 'custodial'
})

// 6. Poll until confirmed
let job
do {
  await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 3000))
  job = await api('GET', `/account/job/${jobId}`)
} while (!['confirmed', 'failed', 'expired'].includes(job.status))
```

## Internal API — Service-to-Service

All /api/internal/* endpoints require:
  Authorization: Bearer <INTERNAL_API_KEY>

### Provision an account (e.g., to sell/gift accounts)
POST /api/internal/provision-account
Body: { username, custodyMode, linkedEmail?, returnKeysOnce?, note? }
Returns: { jobId, userId }

Poll: GET /api/internal/jobs/:jobId
If returnKeysOnce=true and status=confirmed: returns keys exactly once, then wipes.

### Issue a sponsor token (e.g., reward a user in an external app)
POST /api/internal/issue-sponsor-token
Body: { email, note?, expiresInDays? }
Returns: { tokenId, email, expiresAt }

The token is redeemed automatically when the user registers with that email
and calls POST /api/account/create. They bypass the daily quota and the
"previously had account" restriction — but one ACT is still consumed.

## SnapieUser Object

Login endpoints (POST /auth/google, POST /auth/email/login) return this directly
in the response body — no separate GET /auth/me call needed after login:

```
{
  id:           string   — MongoDB ObjectId as hex string
  name:         string|null
  picture:      string|null  — URL (Google accounts only)
  hiveUsername: string|null  — null until account is created
  custodyMode:  "custodial"|"emancipated"|null
  isAdmin:      boolean
}
```

GET /auth/me returns the same shape plus:
  email:                string|null  — from session JWT (cosmetic, not stored)
  accountValueUsd:      number|null  — only present when hiveUsername + custodial
  emancipationRequired: boolean      — only present when above applies

Note: after a fresh login, hiveUsername is null until the user creates a Hive
account via POST /account/create. Always check hiveUsername before using it.

## Endpoints Summary

### Public (no auth)
GET  /health                    → { ok: true }
GET  /quota                     → { total, used, remaining, resetsAt }
GET  /public-config             → { googleClientId, hiveNetwork, discordUrl }
GET  /payment/fee               → { amountHive, amountUsd }
POST /auth/google               → session cookies set + { user: SnapieUser }
POST /auth/apple                → session cookies set + { user: SnapieUser }
POST /auth/email/register       → 202 { pending: true } — sends verification email
POST /auth/email/login          → session cookies set + { user: SnapieUser }
POST /auth/email/resend         → 200 { ok: true }
GET  /auth/email/verify         → redirect to /manage.html (email verification link)

### Session required (cookie)
GET  /auth/me                   → { user: SnapieUser + email + accountValueUsd? + emancipationRequired? }
POST /auth/logout               → clears cookies

GET  /account/eligibility       → { canCreate, canLinkExisting, reason, sponsored? }
GET  /account/check-username/:u → { available, reason? }
POST /account/create            → { jobId, sponsored }   [CSRF]
GET  /account/job/:jobId        → { status, txId, error }
GET  /account/value             → { hiveBalance, hbdBalance, vestingShares, hiveValueUsd, hbdValueUsd, vestingValueUsd, totalValueUsd, emancipationRequired }

POST /payment/hive-intent       → { memo, receivingAccount, amount, amountUsd, expiresAt }   (10/hr)
POST /payment/lightning-intent  → { memo, invoice, amountUsd, expiresAt }                    (10/hr)
GET  /payment/intent/:memo      → { memo, type, status, txId, amountHive, amountUsd, expiresAt }

POST /link/verify-challenge     → { challenge, snapieAccount }  [CSRF]
POST /link/confirm              → { ok, hiveUsername }          [CSRF]

POST /hive/sign-message           → custodial: { signature, account }
                                    emancipated: { needsClientSigning: true, message, account, keyType: "posting" }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/limit-order-create     → custodial: { txId }
                                    emancipated: { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp, account, keyType: "active" }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/limit-order-cancel     → custodial: { txId }
                                    emancipated: { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp, account, keyType: "active" }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/broadcast              → { txId }  (posting ops; always proxied for all users)  [CSRF]
POST /hive/transfer               → custodial: { txId }  emancipated: { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp, account, keyType: "active" }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/transfer-to-savings    → custodial: { txId }  emancipated: { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp, account, keyType: "active" }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/transfer-from-savings  → custodial: { txId }  emancipated: { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp, account, keyType: "active" }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/power-up               → custodial: { txId }  emancipated: { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp, account, keyType: "active" }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/power-down             → custodial: { txId }  emancipated: { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp, account, keyType: "active" }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/delegate               → custodial: { txId }  emancipated: { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp, account, keyType: "active" }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/convert                → custodial: { txId }  emancipated: { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp, account, keyType: "active" }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/collateralized-convert → custodial: { txId }  emancipated: { needsClientSigning: true, unsignedOp, account, keyType: "active" }  [CSRF]
POST /hive/claim-rewards          → { txId }  (posting-level; always proxied)  [CSRF]

GET  /emancipate/status         → { custodyMode, totalUsd, thresholdUsd }
POST /emancipate/start          → { keys: { owner, active, posting, memo }, masterPassword } [CSRF]
                                    masterPassword: single secret all keys derive from (Hive fromLogin); null for pre-master-password accounts

### Internal (Bearer token)
POST /internal/provision-account      → { jobId, userId }
GET  /internal/jobs/:jobId            → { status, ...keys? }
POST /internal/issue-sponsor-token    → { tokenId, email, expiresAt }

### Admin (session + isAdmin required)
GET  /admin/stats               → { actCount, pendingJobs, userCount }
GET  /admin/jobs                → { jobs: [...] }
POST /admin/claim-act           → { ok, txId }           [CSRF]

GET  /admin/sponsor-tokens      → { tokens: [...] }
POST /admin/sponsor-tokens      → { tokenId, emailSent, emailError? }  [CSRF]
  Body: { email, note?, expiresInDays?, sendEmail? }
  sendEmail=true sends an invite email even if user hasn't registered yet.
DELETE /admin/sponsor-tokens/:id → { ok: true }          [CSRF]

GET  /admin/admins              → { admins: [{ userId, name, provider, createdAt }] }
POST /admin/admins              → { userId, name, provider }  [CSRF]
  Body: { email } — user must already be registered
DELETE /admin/admins/:userId    → { ok: true }           [CSRF]
  Cannot demote yourself.

## Error Format

All errors: HTTP 4xx/5xx with body { "error": "error_code_string" }

Common codes:
  unauthorized              — no valid session
  admin_required            — endpoint requires isAdmin
  csrf_invalid              — missing or wrong x-csrf-token
  already_linked            — user already has a Hive account linked
  previously_had_account    — user used their free account already (sponsor token overrides)
  global_daily_limit        — daily free account quota exhausted (resets midnight UTC)
  ip_daily_limit            — per-IP daily quota exhausted
  no_acts                   — Snapie has no pre-claimed account tokens
  invalid_username          — format or on-chain conflict
  insufficient_rc           — HTTP 503; account has no Resource Credits; RC recharges over time
                              For posting ops: the Snapie service account is out of RC (operator issue)
                              For active ops: the user's own account is out of RC
  emancipation_required     — account value exceeded threshold; user must export keys
  not_custodial             — action requires custodial mode
  user_not_found            — no account with that email (admin: grant admin)
  cannot_demote_self        — admin tried to revoke their own admin access

## Apple Sign-In (/auth/apple)

POST /api/auth/apple
Body: { identityToken: string, name?: { firstName: string, lastName?: string } | null }
Returns: { user: SnapieUser }

identityToken: JWT from ASAuthorizationAppleIDCredential.identityToken (iOS native SDK)
name: ONLY send on the user's very first Apple sign-in. iOS only provides name/email once;
      on subsequent logins both are absent from the token. The server preserves the saved
      name automatically — sending null/omitting name on re-login does not wipe it.

Server validates the token against Apple's JWKS (https://appleid.apple.com/auth/keys),
checks iss=https://appleid.apple.com and aud=APPLE_CLIENT_ID (your app's Bundle ID).

Apple may issue a relay email (e.g. xyz@privaterelay.appleid.com) — treat it as opaque.
The stable unique ID is the `sub` claim in the identity token, stored as providerId.

Environment variable: APPLE_CLIENT_ID = your Bundle ID, e.g. "io.snapie.app"
Required by App Store: any app offering third-party login (Google, etc.) must also
offer Sign in with Apple (App Store Review Guideline 4.8).

## Rate Limits (per IP)
  /api/auth/*               30 req / 15 min
  /api/auth/email/resend    5 req / 1 hr
  /api/account/create       5 req / 1 hr
  /api/account/check-*      40 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/sign-message    30 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/limit-order-create       10 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/limit-order-cancel       10 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/broadcast                60 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/transfer                 10 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/transfer-to-savings      10 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/transfer-from-savings    10 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/convert                  10 req / 1 min
  /api/hive/collateralized-convert   10 req / 1 min
  /api/emancipate/*         5 req / 1 hr

## Asset String Format

All monetary amounts are strings — never plain numbers.
  HIVE / HBD:  3 decimal places + currency  →  "1.000 HIVE"  "0.500 HBD"
  VESTS:       6 decimal places + currency  →  "1.000000 VESTS"
Examples of WRONG formats: 1.0, "1", "1.0 HIVE", 1.000, { amount: 1, currency: "HIVE" }

## Active-Level Operations

All /hive/* endpoints except /hive/broadcast and /hive/sign-message are active-level.
All request body fields are camelCase. All endpoints require session cookie + x-csrf-token.
Response pattern:
  Custodial   → { "txId": "abc123..." }
                If the account value exceeds the emancipation threshold, the response also
                includes { "emancipationRequired": true, "accountValueUsd": 27.50 }.
                The operation succeeds regardless — this is a warning, not a block.
                Surface it as: "Your account value has grown. Consider exporting your keys."
  Emancipated → { "needsClientSigning": true, "unsignedOp": ["op_name", {...}], "account": "alice", "keyType": "active" }
                Client must sign unsignedOp via Keychain/AIOha and broadcast independently.
                account and keyType are provided so the app can call:
                  Aioha:   aioha.signAndBroadcastTx(account, [unsignedOp], KeyTypes.Active)
                  Keychain: window.hive_keychain.requestBroadcast(account, [unsignedOp], 'active', cb)

emancipationRequired is also returned by GET /auth/me and GET /account/value.
Check it on page load so you can show a persistent banner without waiting for a transaction.

### POST /hive/transfer
```json
{ "to": "alice", "amount": "1.000 HIVE", "memo": "optional" }
```
  to:     required, 3–16 chars
  amount: required, "N.NNN HIVE" or "N.NNN HBD"
  memo:   optional, max 2048 chars (default "")

### POST /hive/transfer-to-savings
```json
{ "amount": "1.000 HIVE", "to": "alice", "memo": "optional" }
```
  amount: required, "N.NNN HIVE" or "N.NNN HBD"
  to:     optional — defaults to the authenticated user's own account
  memo:   optional, max 2048 chars (default "")
No lockup on deposit. Savings earn interest. Withdrawals take 3 days.

### POST /hive/transfer-from-savings
```json
{ "amount": "1.000 HIVE", "to": "alice", "memo": "optional", "requestId": 1717200000 }
```
  amount:    required, "N.NNN HIVE" or "N.NNN HBD"
  to:        optional — defaults to self
  memo:      optional, max 2048 chars (default "")
  requestId: optional integer — auto-generated from Math.floor(Date.now()/1000) if omitted
             Must be unique per account. Re-using an id for the same account will fail on-chain.
Funds arrive after 3 days.

### POST /hive/convert  (HBD → HIVE)
```json
{ "amount": "1.000 HBD", "requestId": 1717200000 }
```
  amount:    required, "N.NNN HBD" only — NOT HIVE
  requestId: optional integer, auto-generated if omitted
Takes ~3.5 days; settles at the median price feed at that time.

### POST /hive/collateralized-convert  (HIVE → HBD, instant)
```json
{ "amount": "1.000 HIVE", "requestId": 1717200000 }
```
  amount:    required, "N.NNN HIVE" only — NOT HBD
  requestId: optional integer, auto-generated if omitted
HBD delivered to wallet immediately. Excess collateral HIVE returned after ~3.5 days.

### POST /hive/limit-order-create  (internal market trade)
```json
{
  "sell":             "1.000 HIVE",
  "receive":          "0.450 HBD",
  "fillOrKill":       false,
  "expiresInSeconds": 3600,
  "orderId":          1717200000
}
```
  sell:             required, "N.NNN HIVE" or "N.NNN HBD"
  receive:          required, opposite currency to sell — "N.NNN HIVE" or "N.NNN HBD"
  fillOrKill:       optional boolean (default false)
                    false = resting limit order — sits on book until filled or expired
                    true  = instant market swap — cancels immediately if not fully fillable
  expiresInSeconds: optional integer, default 3600, max 2592000 (30 days). Ignored if fillOrKill=true.
  orderId:          optional uint32, auto-generated from timestamp if omitted. Must be unique per account.

IMPORTANT: a fillOrKill=true order that fails to fill still returns { txId } — the transaction
went through but no swap occurred. To confirm a fill, verify account balances changed or
check dhive client.database.findAccountOrders(username, 0, 10) returns no matching orderId.

### POST /hive/limit-order-cancel
```json
{ "orderId": 1717200000 }
```
  orderId: required integer — must match an open order owned by the authenticated user.

### POST /hive/power-up
```json
{ "amount": "1.000 HIVE" }
```
Stakes HIVE as Hive Power. Amount must be "N.NNN HIVE" (3 decimals).

### POST /hive/power-down
```json
{ "amount": "1.000000 VESTS" }
```
Amount must be "N.NNNNNN VESTS" (6 decimals) — NOT HIVE. Unstaking takes 13 weeks.
To convert HP → VESTS: vests = hp * total_vesting_shares / total_vesting_fund_hive
(from dhive client.database.getDynamicGlobalProperties())

### POST /hive/delegate
```json
{ "delegatee": "alice", "amount": "1.000000 VESTS" }
```
  delegatee: required, target account username
  amount:    required, "N.NNNNNN VESTS" — set to "0.000000 VESTS" to remove delegation

### POST /hive/claim-rewards
No request body. Server reads pending rewards from the chain automatically.
Returns { txId } on success.

## Posting-Level Broadcast (/hive/broadcast)

Allowed op types: vote, comment, delete_comment, custom_json, claim_reward_balance
Request body: { "op": ["op_type", { ...fields }] }
The op field is a 2-element array — NOT { op: "comment", ...fields }, NOT [["comment", {...}]].

Example — root post:
```json
{
  "op": ["comment", {
    "parent_author":  "",
    "parent_permlink": "hive",
    "author":          "alice",
    "permlink":        "my-post-slug",
    "title":           "My Post Title",
    "body":            "Post content here.",
    "json_metadata":   "{\"tags\":[\"hive\"],\"app\":\"myapp/1.0\"}"
  }]
}
```
  parent_author:  "" for a root post; the replied-to author for a comment
  json_metadata:  must be a JSON-encoded STRING, not an object
  author:         must match the authenticated user's hiveUsername

Example — reply: same as above but parent_author = "bob", parent_permlink = "their-post-slug", title = ""

Example — vote:
```json
{ "op": ["vote", { "voter": "alice", "author": "bob", "permlink": "some-post", "weight": 10000 }] }
```
  weight: -10000 to 10000 (10000 = 100% upvote, -10000 = full downvote, 0 = remove vote)

Example — custom_json:
```json
{
  "op": ["custom_json", {
    "required_auths":         [],
    "required_posting_auths": ["alice"],
    "id":                     "myapp",
    "json":                   "{\"action\":\"follow\",\"target\":\"bob\"}"
  }]
}
```
  json: must be a JSON-encoded STRING, max 8192 bytes

## Message Signing (/hive/sign-message)

```json
{ "message": "your-challenge-string" }
```
  message: required string, max 512 chars

Custodial response:   { "signature": "abc...", "account": "alice" }
Emancipated response: { "needsClientSigning": true, "message": "...", "account": "alice", "keyType": "posting" }

Signs with SHA-256 + secp256k1 (dhive cryptoUtils.sha256 + PrivateKey.sign).
Compatible with hive-js / dhive verifySignature.

Typical challenge-response flow:
  1. Your app generates a nonce (UUID or timestamp string)
  2. POST /api/hive/sign-message { message: nonce }
  3. Verify: verifySignature(account, nonce, signature, onChainPostingPubKey)
  4. On success: grant access

Emancipated client signing:
  Keychain: window.hive_keychain.requestSignBuffer(account, message, 'Posting', cb)
  AIOha:    aioha.signMessage(message, KeyTypes.Posting)

The response always includes account and keyType so the app needs no additional context.

## Cookies
  snapieauth_session   httpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Lax  — JWT RS256
  snapieauth_csrf      Secure, SameSite=Lax (JS-readable) — CSRF token
